---
title: "RERconverge Analysis"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup-knitr, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

## 1. Load packages and input data

```{r libraries-data}
library(RERconverge)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ComplexUpset)
library(wesanderson)
library(ComplexUpset)
library(ggplot2)
library(wesanderson)
library(enrichplot)
library(clusterProfiler)

setwd("~/Documents/RERConverge")

# Phenotype table: column 1 = species name, column 2 = phenotype category
pheno = read.csv("HelderPhenotypes_3cat.csv", header = FALSE)

# Species used as the foreground set for RER/phenotype correlation
# (first foreground group)
foreground = c(
  "Rattus_norvegicus",
  "Mus_musculus",
  "Mus_spretus",
  "Mus_caroli",
  "Mus_pahari",
  "Acomys_cahirinus",
  "Cricetomys_gambianus"
)

# Second foreground group, used for the 2-vs-3 comparison / plots
foreground2 = c(
  "Meriones_unguiculatus",
  "Psammomys_obesus",
  "Microtus_ochrogaster",
  "Ondatra_zibethicus",
  "Ellobius_talpinus",
  "Ellobius_lutescens",
  "Sigmodon_hispidus",
  "Nannospalax_galili",
  "Jaculus_jaculus",
  "Allactaga_bullata",
  "Dipodomys_stephensi",
  "Dipodomys_ordii",
  "Castor_canadensis",
  "Aplodontia_rufa"
)
```

## 2. Helper: summarize a pairwise phenotype comparison

```{r summarize-comparison-helper}
# Prints a short summary (top hits, best peak, significant/NA counts) for a
# single pairwise result table, and returns the row with the lowest p-value.
summarize_comparison = function(res_pair, label) {
  message(paste0("Top 5 results for ", label, ":"))
  print(head(res_pair[order(res_pair$P, na.last = TRUE), ], 5))

  # Row with the smallest p-value = strongest peak for this comparison
  best_peak = res_pair[which.min(res_pair$P), ]
  message(paste0(
    "Best peak for ", label, ": ", rownames(best_peak),
    " with p-value: ", best_peak$P, " and Rho: ", best_peak$Rho
  ))

  message(paste0("Count of significant p-values (p < 0.05) in ", label, ":"))
  print(table(res_pair$P < 0.05))

  message(paste0("Count of NA values in ", label, ":"))
  print(table(is.na(res_pair$P)))

  best_peak
}
```

## 3. getStats(): read tree, compute RERs, correlate with phenotype

```{r getstats-function}
# Main analysis function:
#  - reads the tree file
#  - computes relative evolutionary rates (RERs)
#  - converts the categorical phenotype into tree paths
#  - correlates RERs with the phenotype, globally and pairwise
getStats = function(tree_file, pheno, plot_avg = FALSE, check_pairs = TRUE) {

  message("Reading tree from file...")
  tree = readTrees(tree_file)
  message("Tree file read.")

  message("Calculating RERs...")
  mamRER = getAllResiduals(tree, transform = "sqrt", use.weights = TRUE, norm = "scale")
  str(mamRER)

  if (plot_avg) {
    message("Plotting average tree...")
    avgtree = plotTreeHighlightBranches(
      tree$masterTree,
      hlspecies = c("Mus_musculus", "Mesocricetus_auratus", "Acomys_cahirinus"),
      hlcols = c("black", "green"),
      main = "Average tree"
    )
    print(avgtree)
  }

  message("Preparing phenotypic data...")
  # Turn the two-column data frame into a named vector: names = species, values = phenotype
  pheno = setNames(pheno$V2, pheno$V1)
  print(head(pheno))

  message("Converting categorical phenotypes to paths...")
  charpaths = char2PathsCategorical(pheno, tree)
  str(charpaths)

  message("Correlating RERs with categorical phenotypes...")
  # res[[1]] = global (all-category) result, res[[2]] = named list of pairwise results
  res = correlateWithCategoricalPhenotype(mamRER, charpaths, sort = TRUE)
  str(res)

  res_global = res[[1]]

  message("Top 5 results for global p-values:")
  print(head(res_global, 5))

  message("Count of significant p-values (p < 0.05) in global results:")
  print(table(res_global$P < 0.05))

  message("Count of NA values in global results:")
  print(table(is.na(res_global$P)))

  best_peak = res_global[which.min(res_global$P), ]
  message(paste(
    "Best peak (lowest p-value):", rownames(best_peak),
    "with p-value:", best_peak$P, "and Rho:", best_peak$Rho
  ))

  # Placeholders for the pairwise results (only filled in if check_pairs = TRUE)
  res_1v2 = res_1v3 = res_2v3 = NULL
  best_peak_1v2 = best_peak_1v3 = best_peak_2v3 = NULL

  if (check_pairs) {
    # Pull out each pairwise comparison from the categorical result list
    res_1v2 = res[[2]][["1 - 2"]]
    res_1v3 = res[[2]][["1 - 3"]]
    res_2v3 = res[[2]][["2 - 3"]]

    best_peak_1v2 = summarize_comparison(res_1v2, "murine vs brachydont")
    best_peak_1v3 = summarize_comparison(res_1v3, "murine vs hypsodont")
    best_peak_2v3 = summarize_comparison(res_2v3, "brachydont vs hypsodont")
  }

  return(list(
    best_peak = rownames(best_peak),
    mamRER = mamRER,
    res = res,
    res_global = res_global,
    tree = tree,
    res_1v2 = res_1v2,
    res_1v3 = res_1v3,
    res_2v3 = res_2v3,
    best_peak_1v2 = if (!is.null(best_peak_1v2)) rownames(best_peak_1v2) else NULL,
    best_peak_1v3 = if (!is.null(best_peak_1v3)) rownames(best_peak_1v3) else NULL,
    best_peak_2v3 = if (!is.null(best_peak_2v3)) rownames(best_peak_2v3) else NULL
  ))
}
```

## 4. plotRERs(): plot RERs for the peaks of interest

```{r plotrers-function}
# Generates RER plots (and optionally a tree view) for the global best peak
# and for each pairwise-comparison best peak. If `stats` is not supplied,
# getStats() is called internally to produce it.
plotRERs = function(tree_file, pheno, foreground, stats = NULL,
                     peak = NULL, peak_1v2 = NULL, peak_1v3 = NULL, peak_2v3 = NULL,
                     plot_RERs = TRUE, plot_tree = TRUE, plot_peak = TRUE) {

  if (is.null(stats)) stats = getStats(tree_file, pheno)
  mamRER = stats$mamRER
  tree   = stats$tree

  # Fall back to the best peaks found by getStats() when none is specified
  if (is.null(peak)) {
    message("No peak specified, using the best peak from getStats...")
    peak = stats$best_peak
  }
  if (is.null(peak_1v2)) {
    message("No peak specified for 1v2, using the best peak from getStats...")
    peak_1v2 = stats$best_peak_1v2
  }
  if (is.null(peak_1v3)) {
    message("No peak specified for 1v3, using the best peak from getStats...")
    peak_1v3 = stats$best_peak_1v3
  }
  if (is.null(peak_2v3)) {
    message("No peak specified for 2v3, using the best peak from getStats...")
    peak_2v3 = stats$best_peak_2v3
  }

  message("Converting foreground to paths...")
  # Terminal-branch paths for each foreground species group, used to color plots
  phenvExample = foreground2Paths(foreground, tree, clade = "terminal")
  phenv2 = foreground2Paths(foreground2, tree, clade = "terminal")

  plot_RER = plot_RER_1v2 = plot_RER_1v3 = plot_RER_2v3 = NULL
  rers    = NULL
  rertree = NULL

  if (plot_RERs) {
    par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

    message(paste("Plotting RERs for peak:", peak))
    plot_RER = plotRers(mamRER, peak, phenv = phenvExample)

    message(paste("Plotting RERs for 1v2 peak:", peak_1v2))
    plot_RER_1v2 = plotRers(mamRER, peak_1v2, phenv = phenvExample)

    message(paste("Plotting RERs for 1v3 peak:", peak_1v3))
    plot_RER_1v3 = plotRers(mamRER, peak_1v3, phenv = phenvExample)

    message(paste("Plotting RERs for 2v3 peak:", peak_2v3))
    plot_RER_2v3 = plotRers(mamRER, peak_2v3, phenv = phenv2)
  }

  if (plot_tree) {
    message("Returning RERs as a tree...")
    par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
    rers = returnRersAsTree(tree, mamRER, peak, plot = TRUE, phenv = phenvExample)
  }

  if (plot_peak) {
    message(paste("Plotting tree for peak:", peak))
    par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
    rertree = plotTreeHighlightBranches(
      tree$trees[[peak]],
      hlspecies = foreground,
      main = paste("Tree of", peak)
    )
  }

  return(list(
    plot_RER = plot_RER,
    plot_RER_1v2 = plot_RER_1v2,
    plot_RER_1v3 = plot_RER_1v3,
    plot_RER_2v3 = plot_RER_2v3,
    rers = rers,
    RERtree = rertree
  ))
}
```

## 5. Run the main analysis

```{r run-main-analysis}
# Run the full pipeline on the full tree, including the average-tree plot
res = getStats(tree_file = "tree_full.txt", pheno = pheno, plot_avg = TRUE)

# Generate RER plots for the peaks identified above
rer_plots = plotRERs(tree_file = "tree_full.txt", pheno, foreground, stats = res)

# Global best-peak RER plot, recolored and labeled
RER_global = rer_plots$plot_RER
RER = RER_global + ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c("black", "forestgreen")) + ggtitle("PLXNA2_Distal_Intergenic")
RER

# Best peak for the 2-vs-3 comparison, recolored and labeled
RER_global = rer_plots$plot_RER_2v3
RER_2v3 = RER_global + ggplot2::scale_colour_manual(values = c("black", "orange")) + ggtitle("VANGL1_Promoter")
RER_2v3
```

## 6. UpSet plot of significant CREs per pairwise comparison

```{r upset-plot, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Drop rows with no p-value before selecting significant hits
res_1v2 = res$res_1v2 %>% filter(!is.na(P))
res_1v3 = res$res_1v3 %>% filter(!is.na(P))
res_2v3 = res$res_2v3 %>% filter(!is.na(P))

# Significant (p < 0.05) CRE names for each pairwise comparison
sig_1v2 = rownames(res_1v2[res_1v2$P < 0.05, ])
sig_1v3 = rownames(res_1v3[res_1v3$P < 0.05, ])
sig_2v3 = rownames(res_2v3[res_2v3$P < 0.05, ])

gene_list = list(
  "murine vs brachydont" = sig_1v2,
  "murine vs hypsodont" = sig_1v3,
  "brachydont vs hypsodont" = sig_2v3
)

library(stringr)

# each category present
annot_patterns = c("Distal_Intergenic", "Promoter", "Intron", "Exon", "5_UTR", "3_UTR")

get_annotation = function(x) {
  # Look for the first matching category
  ann = str_extract(x, paste(annot_patterns, collapse = "|"))
  ann
}

all_genes = unique(c(sig_1v2, sig_1v3, sig_2v3))

# Membership table used to build the UpSet plot: one row per CRE, one logical
# column per pairwise comparison, plus its genomic annotation category
df = data.frame(
  gene = all_genes,
  "Découplé vs Ancestral" = all_genes %in% sig_1v2,
  "Découplé vs Couplé" = all_genes %in% sig_1v3,
  "Ancestral vs Couplé"= all_genes %in% sig_2v3,
  check.names = FALSE
)

df$Annotation = get_annotation(df$gene)

sets = c("Découplé vs Ancestral",
  "Découplé vs Couplé",
  "Ancestral vs Couplé")

pal = wes_palette("Zissou1", 6, type = "continuous")

# Intersection sizes colored by annotation category
upset(
  df,
  sets,
  base_annotations = list(
    'Intersection size' = intersection_size(
      counts = TRUE,
      mapping = aes(fill = Annotation)
    ) + scale_fill_manual(values = pal)
  ),
  width_ratio = 0.15
)
```

## 7. GO enrichment on globally significant genes

```{r go-enrichment}
# Extract the gene symbol (text before the first underscore) from each
# significant CRE name, then title-case it to match org.Mm.eg.db's SYMBOL keys
signif = rownames(res$res_global)[which(res$res_global$P < 0.05)]
genes = sapply(signif, function(x) strsplit(x, "_")[[1]][1])
genes = unique(genes)
genes = paste0(toupper(substr(genes, 1, 1)), tolower(substr(genes, 2, nchar(genes))))

# Background gene universe = every gene tested (not just significant ones)
all_genes = rownames(res$res_global)
universe = sapply(all_genes, function(x) strsplit(x, "_")[[1]][1])
universe = unique(universe)
universe = paste0(toupper(substr(universe, 1, 1)), tolower(substr(universe, 2, nchar(universe))))

# GO Biological Process enrichment, restricted to the tested-gene universe
go = enrichGO(genes, OrgDb = "org.Mm.eg.db", ont = "BP", keyType = "SYMBOL", universe = universe)
go_plot = dotplot(go)
go_plot
```
